translation.en: I do not want to break the party.

notes:
phá = to destroy or break something so it’s damaged or ruined. It can be physical (objects, buildings) or more abstract (plans, mood).

It’s a verb, so you use it directly with an object: you phá something. It often combines with nouns like phá hỏng (break/ruin), phá vỡ (break apart), or phá kỷ lục (break a record).

It can also mean “mess up” or “spoil” something, not just physically destroy it. Close synonym: làm hỏng (make broken); opposite ideas: sửa (fix), xây (build).
notes_md:
**phá** = to destroy or break something so it’s damaged or ruined. It can be physical (objects, buildings) or more abstract (plans, mood).

It’s a verb, so you use it directly with an object: you *phá* something. It often combines with nouns like **phá hỏng** (break/ruin), **phá vỡ** (break apart), or **phá kỷ lục** (break a record).


It can also mean “mess up” or “spoil” something, not just physically destroy it. Close synonym: **làm hỏng** (make broken); opposite ideas: **sửa** (fix), **xây** (build).
exampleSentencesTranslated: You should not destroy other people's toys because that is not good.
He destroyed the old watch to see what was inside.
If we destroy the rules, we will be punished very seriously.

example.vi: Họ đã phá vỡ cửa sổ.

definitions.vi: phá vỡ hoặc phá hủy một cái gì đó

exampleSentences: Em không nên phá đồ chơi của người khác vì như vậy không tốt.
Anh ấy đã phá cái đồng hồ cũ để xem bên trong có gì.
Nếu mình phá luật lệ, sẽ bị phạt rất nặng đấy.
